title: From Genomic Data to Clinical Insight
text:
This project outlines how I approach the design of a reproducible analysis workflow that connects genomic data processing, statistical analysis, and interpretable reporting.


Raw Data → Quality Control → Processing → Statistical Analysis → Interpretation → Reporting



Clinical and research workflows need more than analysis alone. They also require data validation, reproducibility, and outputs that can be understood by collaborators with different backgrounds.





Checking data quality, missingness, and consistency before downstream analysis.



Using statistical methods appropriate to the study design and research question.



Placing findings in context, including limitations and practical relevance.



Presenting outputs in a form that supports communication, review, and reuse.

title: About
text:
I recently completed an MSc in Medical Genetics at the University of British Columbia. My background combines genomics, statistical analysis, scientific communication, and collaborative research.



R

HTML/CSS

I am interested in bioinformatics, biostatistics, reproducible workflows, and building tools that make complex analyses easier to interpret and use.

title: Home
text:
I am an MSc graduate in Medical Genetics with experience in genomics, statistical analysis, and research tool development.
My work sits at the intersection of bioinformatics, biostatistics, and scientific communication. I am especially interested in building reproducible analytical workflows, translating complex datasets into interpretable results, and creating tools that support research and clinical decision-making.


From Genomic Data to Clinical Insight




Genomics and health data analysis
Statistical reasoning and interpretation
Research tool and workflow design
Clear communication for technical and non-technical audiences
